Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search emails for the Heartland Q1 invoice and dispute history using `search_emails` (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026" or "overbilling" or "dispute" or similar). Find: Jake Loomis's invoice submission (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 for 8 moves), Chloe's overbilling analysis ($4,800 in invalid charges), Chloe's forwarded cancellation evidence, and Marcus's formal dispute letter to Jake.
OE2: Search emails for cancellation notices sent to Heartland using `search_emails` (query: "cancellation Heartland" or "Marchetti" or "Osei" or "cancelled" or similar). Find: Chloe's cancellation email for Lily Marchetti on Feb 10 (email_cancel_marchetti_heartland) and Danielle Osei on Feb 13 (email_cancel_osei_heartland), both sent to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com.
OE3: Search emails for any response from Heartland to the cancellation notices or formal dispute using `search_emails` (query: "jake.loomis" or "Heartland" or similar, checking INBOX). Discovery: no reply exists from Jake Loomis or Heartland to either the Feb 10/13 cancellation emails or the March 25 formal dispute letter.
OE4: Search emails for the damage incident and insurance gap details using `search_emails` (query: "damage" or "prototype" or "incident report" or "Mosaic" or "insurance" or similar). Find: Tamara Byrd's damage complaint (URGENT, $90K prototype Unit #1), Emeka's acknowledgment, and Emeka's full incident report disclosing the $40K insurance gap, the remote sign-off root cause, the Macon depot overnight stop, and the intern delivery receipt.
OE5: Search emails for any communication between MoveOps and Heartland about the prototype damage or $40K liability gap using `search_emails` (query: "Heartland" or "liability" or "damage" or "jake.loomis" or similar). Discovery: no correspondence exists between MoveOps and Heartland regarding the prototype damage, the $40K insurance gap, or any liability recovery — the vendor has neither been formally notified about the damage claim via email nor responded to the situation.
OE6: Search Airtable for relocation records to verify Q1 bill line items using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name" or "Company", value: employee names or client names from the invoice). Find: 5 completed moves (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves), 2 cancelled moves (Marchetti status: Cancelled, Osei status: Cancelled), and Venkatesh's record showing vendor as Swift Relocations (not Heartland).
OE7: Search Airtable for all April relocations assigned to Heartland using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator" or "Status" = "In Progress", and filtering for Heartland as vendor) or `airtable_list_records` to scan April moves. Find: Zara Okoye (Mosaic, completed/damaged), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, split), Emilia Cruz (NorthWind, household), Marcus Webb (Canopy, split), Joaquín Reyes (GreenStack, 900-lb rig).
OE8: Search QuickBooks for Heartland Movers bills using `quickbooks_search_bills` (criteria: "Heartland" or similar). Find: bill_heartland_q1_2026 ($12,800 original), bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold ($12,800 on hold with disputed annotations), bill_heartland_abdi ($2,150 for individual Abdi move), and bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 contingent liability accrual, ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC).
OE9: Search QuickBooks for the damage-related credit memo and accounts using `quickbooks_search_invoices` (criteria: "Mosaic" or "damage" or "CM-2026" or similar) and/or `quickbooks_search_accounts` (criteria: "liability" or "contingent" or "damage" or similar). Find: credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 (-$90,000, CM-2026-0415) and account acct_contingent_liability_claims ($90,000 balance).
OE10: Search CRM for client deals affected by April Heartland relocations using `crm_search_deals` (dealname: "Mosaic" or "Vectral" or "NorthWind" or "GreenStack" or "Canopy" or similar) or `crm_search_companies` (name: client names). Find deal amounts needed for margin calculation: Mosaic Okoye ($2,700), Vectral April ($15,900), NorthWind Denver expansion ($14,700), GreenStack April ($9,440).
OE11: Search Slack for internal discussions about Heartland using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "vendor dispute" or "payment hold" or similar). Find: Chloe's Q1 invoice alert, her detailed reconciliation findings, Marcus's payment hold confirmation, his formal dispute confirmation, Hana's reconciliation tracker update, and the creation of tracking ticket OPS-342.
OE12: Search Slack #finance channel for financial discussions using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "Heartland" or "vendor" or similar) or `conversations_history` (channel_id: "C005"). Find: Marcus's full vendor payment summary comparing Q1 to Q4 2025, and Hana's VRECON-2026-Q1-HM tracker entry noting this is the first billing problem with Heartland.
OE13: Search Linear for the vendor dispute tracking issue using `linear_list_issues` (query: "Heartland" or "vendor" or "reconciliation" or similar). Find: issue_heartland_q1_recon (OPS-342), titled "Vendor invoice reconciliation -- Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)", assigned to Chloe Vance, state: in_progress, due: April 10, labels: vendor-dispute, reconciliation, heartland-movers, finance.
OE14: Get detailed comments on OPS-342 using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: "issue_heartland_q1_recon") to find any progress notes or updates.
OE15: Look up Marcus Thorne's and Elena Rostova's email addresses using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Marcus Thorne") and `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Elena Rostova"). Find: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE16: Update the Linear issue OPS-342 with comprehensive findings using `linear_create_comment` (issueId: "issue_heartland_q1_recon", body: summarizing Q1 reconciliation confirmation, damage liability exposure, no vendor response to the dispute or the damage situation, and April reassignment analysis).
OE17: Update QuickBooks financial records to reflect real liabilities using `quickbooks_update_account` (account_id: "acct_contingent_liability_claims" or similar, patch: updated description or notes reflecting the $40K uninsured exposure and $4,800 dispute status) or `quickbooks_create_bill` for a corrected vendor bill.
OE18: Send a comprehensive breakdown email using `send_email` (sender: "marcus.thorne@moveops.com", recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], cc: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"] or recipients including both, subject: relating to Heartland Movers financial position / vendor strategy, content: full Q1 dispute breakdown, damage exposure, April reassignment analysis, internal concerns, and recommendation context).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via send_email) with recipients, or CC, including both [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com) and [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com), and the tool returned success.
outcome

Elena is in recipients but Marcus is only the sender, so the criterion is not fully met.

Marcus is the sender only — he is NOT in the recipients or CC fields. The criterion requires both addresses appear in recipients/CC. FAIL.

✓Run #6The send_email tool was called with recipients ['marcus.thorne@moveops.com', 'elena.rostova@moveops.com'] and returned a success response with email_id 'b6f598515c0843eb88bb7a2687e2ab58'.
2 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) identifies the Q1 bill as HM-2026-Q1-0042 for $12,800 covering 8 line items (or similar invoice reference and total).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body clearly identifies the Q1 bill as 'Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042' for '$12,800 (8 moves × $1,600)' with a line-by-line breakdown of all 8 items. All required details are present.
✓Run #2The email body in PART 1 explicitly states 'Heartland billed us $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each' and references the invoice number 'HM-2026-Q1-0042' in the section header.
✓Run #3The email body under 'SECTION 1: Q1 BILL VERIFICATION (HM-2026-Q1-0042)' explicitly states 'Heartland billed $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each.' Both the invoice reference and total are present.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states: 'Heartland submitted invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 for $12,800 (8 moves × $1,600). I verified every line item individually...' — invoice number, total, and 8 line items are all present.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states 'Heartland submitted bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 for 8 moves at $1,600 each = $12,800.' All three identifiers (invoice number, total, and 8 line items) are present.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly references 'invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042' and states '$12,800 (8 moves × $1,600)' in Section 1 of the email content.
3 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) states that 5 of 8 line items are legitimate, totaling $8,000 (or a similar breakdown confirming 5 verified moves).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states '✅ VERIFIED — 5 LEGITIMATE MOVES ($8,000)' and lists all five verified moves (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves), confirming 5 of 8 line items are legitimate totaling $8,000.
✓Run #2The email body contains 'VERIFIED MOVES (5 × $1,600 = $8,000 — we legitimately owe this)' and lists all 5 confirmed legitimate moves explicitly.
✓Run #3The email clearly lists 'VERIFIED — VALID ($8,000)' with 5 named moves (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves), confirming 5 of 8 line items are legitimate at $8,000 total.
✓Run #4The email body includes 'VERIFIED LEGITIMATE (5 moves = $8,000)' and lists all 5 moves individually, confirming 5 of 8 line items are legitimate at $1,600 each = $8,000.
✓Run #5The email body clearly states '✅ VERIFIED (5 moves = $8,000)' and lists all 5 verified moves at $1,600 each, totaling $8,000.
✓Run #6The email body states 'Verified legitimate: $8,000 (5 moves)' and lists all 5 verified moves (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves) in Section 1.
4 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) identifies the Lily Marchetti move as disputed because it was cancelled, with the cancellation sent on or around February 10, 2026.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body identifies 'Lily Marchetti — Nashville → Chicago (Canopy Health) — $1,600' as disputed because the 'Move was CANCELLED' and explicitly states 'Cancellation email sent to Jake Loomis on Feb 10, 2026.' This matches the criterion.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'Lily Marchetti — Nashville → Chicago (Canopy Health) — $1,600 ❌ MOVE WAS CANCELLED... Cancellation email sent to Jake Loomis on Feb 10, 2026. No reply ever received. Airtable RELOC-2026-009 status: Cancelled.'
✓Run #3The email identifies 'Lily Marchetti — Nashville→Chicago (Canopy Health) — RELOC-2026-009 — CANCELLED Feb 10' and states 'Chloe Vance sent cancellation to Jake Loomis on Feb 10. No acknowledgment.'
✓Run #4The email body states: 'Lily Marchetti — Nashville → Chicago (Canopy Health) — $1,600. STATUS: CANCELLED. Employee decided not to relocate. Cancellation emailed to Jake Loomis on Feb 10. NEVER ACKNOWLEDGED.' Both the cancellation reason and Feb 10 date are present.
